title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-based training completion
exclusions: Does NOT assess training effectiveness; attests that role-required modules are completed within policy cadence by each person in the role.
evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_7_2_competence' (daily) emits per person: user, role, required_modules, completed_modules, compliance_pct, to compliance_summary.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A user missing a required module shows compliance_pct<100 in the next daily run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A user with all required modules completed within cadence is absent.

dataSources: `index=hr` sourcetype=lms:completion; `index=hr` sourcetype=hr:role_assignment; required-module matrix `iso_27001_training_matrix.csv`. (Splunkbase 1621 — Splunk CIM Add-on)
spl:
index=hr sourcetype=hr:role_assignment earliest=-30d
| stats latest(role) AS role BY user
| lookup iso_27001_training_matrix.csv role OUTPUT required_modules
| eval required_modules=split(required_modules,",")
| join user
  [search index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion status=completed earliest=-365d
   | stats values(module) AS completed_modules BY user]
| eval covered=mvmap(required_modules, if(isnotnull(mvfind(completed_modules,x)),1,0))
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*sum(covered)/mvcount(required_modules),0)
| where compliance_pct<100
| table user role required_modules completed_modules compliance_pct
description: Joins the role-to-module matrix against LMS completions to report each person's coverage. Clause 7.2 requires the organisation to determine and maintain competence.
value: Replaces the annual LMS export with a daily compliance signal. HR can notify managers proactively rather than after the audit finding.
implementation: (1) Onboard LMS completions; (2) maintain iso_27001_training_matrix.csv per role; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) compliance_pct<100 triggers an HR email; (5) quarterly report to ISMS committee. Indexes required: `index=hr`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=hr:role_assignment`, `sourcetype=hr:role_assignment;`, `sourcetype=lms:completion`, `sourcetype=lms:completion;`.
visualization: Stacked bar by role, table of sub-100% users, single value 'users below 100%'.
knownFalsePositives:
1. **Assessment cycle alignment** — Control assessments following annual cycles may show brief non-current periods between assessment completion and report publication.

2. **Finding remediation within SLA** — Open findings within their documented remediation timeline are being addressed per policy, not neglected or overlooked.

3. **Framework version transitions** — Migration between control framework versions (ISO 27001:2013 to 2022) creates temporary mapping gaps during the documented transition period.

4. **Accepted residual risk items** — Findings formally accepted through the risk management governance process with documented compensating controls and management sign-off.

5. **Evidence collection cadence differences** — Controls with different evidence generation frequencies (real-time vs. quarterly) show varying currency in compliance dashboards.

**Operational suppression:** Maintain a lookup table (`iso27001_approved_exceptions.csv`) mapping known legitimate activities by control owner, risk acceptance reference, and expiry date. Filter the SPL results against this lookup to suppress documented exceptions and reduce alert fatigue. Review and rotate entries at each ISMS management review cycle.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `hr` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `iso_27001_training_matrix.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('iso_27001_training_matrix.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `7.2`. The control objective is: "Evidence that ISO/IEC 27001 7.2 (Competence) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.53: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-based training completion." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `hr` under `hr:role_assignment`, `lms:completion`, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Onboard LMS completions. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 2:** maintain iso_27001_training_matrix.csv per role. The lookup table identified here is a long-lived compliance artifact; version it in source control and track every change with a pull-request review.

• **Hint 3:** schedule UC daily. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** compliance_pct<100 triggers an HR email. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** quarterly report to ISMS committee. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-based training completion," the aggregation is the evidence artifact itself — auditors will read these rows as the official record of ISO/IEC 27001 control activity.

This search correlates the outer dataset (index=`hr`, sourcetype=`hr:role_assignment`) with a subsearch on `user` across the last 30 days. `type=inner` means the search only returns rows where `user` exists in both datasets. This is the correct choice when the detection requires presence in both sources — a left-join would include orphaned rows that cannot be evaluated against the compliance rule.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.6.53: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-ba]
cron_schedule = 0 3 1 * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.6.53,reg=ISO/IEC_27001
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=hr sourcetype=hr:role_assignment earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `user`, `role`, `module`, `status`, `_time`. Run:

```spl
index=hr sourcetype=hr:role_assignment earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table user, role, module, status, _time
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `iso_27001_training_matrix.csv`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup iso_27001_training_matrix.csv
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(d) Threshold calibration:** Before enabling as a production alert, run the base search with the threshold removed and profile the distribution of `compliance_pct`:

```spl
<base search> earliest=-30d
| stats perc50(compliance_pct) as p50 perc90(compliance_pct) as p90 perc99(compliance_pct) as p99 max(compliance_pct) as hi
```

The current threshold `100` should sit between p90 and p99 for a balanced signal-to-noise ratio. If it's below p50, expect heavy alert volume; if above p99, expect under-detection.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.6.53: ISO/IEC 27001 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Stacked Bar By Role (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Table Of Sub-100% Users (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Single Value 'Users Below 100%' (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**ISO/IEC 27001-specific failure modes:**

• Statement of Applicability (SoA) drift: ISO 27001 requires a SoA listing which Annex A controls are applicable and which are excluded with justification. A detection for a control excluded in the SoA adds noise to the audit evidence pile; reconcile UC-to-SoA mapping annually. Maintain `iso27001_soa.csv` with current applicability.

• Context of the organisation (Cl. 4): controls calibrated for a 50-person company's threat model do not suit a 5000-person multinational's. Detection thresholds, log retention, and escalation paths must align with the organisation's ISMS scope — a single 'enterprise default' for ISO 27001 is a tell-tale sign of a cookie-cutter implementation.

• Internal audit programme (Cl. 9.2) evidence: detections that support internal audits must produce records that can be attached to audit evidence packs. Tag the alert payload with audit_cycle=YYYY-HN so evidence bundling is trivial.

• Management review inputs (Cl. 9.3): control effectiveness metrics must be reviewable at management review. A detection that only raises alerts (no trend metric) cannot be brought to the management meeting; schedule a companion report that produces trend data per control.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-based training completion. ISO 27001 tells us what good security management looks like, and we document that we follow it.
